_comment: Manually maintained anchor. Verify each QID at https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q<ID> before running the pipeline. HIGH-confidence QIDs are marked; MODERATE ones must be checked.

_confidence_notes.HIGH: bouillabaisse, tarte-tatin, ratatouille-nouvelle, ratatouille-contemporary, gratin-dauphinois, madeleine, peche-melba, vol-au-vent, consomme, crepes-suzette, tournedos-rossini, pain-perdu, foie-gras-contemporary, soupe-de-truffe, bechamel-careme, sauce-bechamel-escoffier, salade-nicoise, mousse-chocolat, hypocras, blanc-manger, potage, dariole, charlotte-russe

hypocras.notes: Updated from Q869392; Q673389 is hippocrass — wine mixed with sugar and spices

blanc-manger.notes: Updated from Q473094; Q551514 is canonical blancmange dessert item

potage.notes: Updated from Q192783; Q5420610 is the food item for potage

oublie.notes: Updated from Q1429839; Q1226651 is wafer cookie (thin type of biscuit) — closest food match

dariole.notes: Updated from Q1159456; Q3016156 is specifically a French pastry and dessert mold

farcement.notes: Updated from Q3069434; Q3066793 is the Farcement item (no description yet on Wikidata). ERA MISMATCH expected: Savoie dish ~19th century

consomme.notes: Updated from Q191469; Q2082064 is Consommé — type of clear soup

vol-au-vent.notes: Updated from Q897523; Q304868 is the puff pastry food item

charlotte-russe.notes: Updated from Q1064089; Q2961045 is specifically charlotte russe (not the parent Charlotte dessert category)

bechamel-careme.notes: Updated from Q178820; Q209974 is béchamel — sauce of French cuisine. Shares QID with sauce-bechamel-escoffier

gelee-royale.notes: Updated from Q14947524 (no French label); Q25383981 confirmed via Wikipedia cross-link. Carême's gelée royale is a culinary aspic/gelée.

sauce-bechamel-escoffier.notes: Updated from Q178820; Q209974 is béchamel — sauce of French cuisine. Shares QID with bechamel-careme — pipeline handles duplicate QIDs via by_dish_id index

peche-melba.notes: Updated from Q729473; Q1567954 is Peach Melba — type of dessert

tournedos-rossini.notes: Updated from Q1076224; Q2303610 is Tournedos Rossini — French steak dish

bouillabaisse.notes: Updated from Q190174; Q828043 is traditional Provençal fish stew. ERA MISMATCH risk: ancient Provençal dish

crepes-suzette.notes: Updated from Q1141416; Q1142539 is Crêpe Suzette — French pancake dessert. Attribution contested between Escoffier and Henri Carpentier

ratatouille-nouvelle.notes: Updated from Q178115; Q183095 is ratatouille — French dish. Canonical dish QID shared with ratatouille-contemporary

salade-nicoise.notes: Updated from Q697973; Q209486 is Niçard salad — composed tomato, egg, fish and vegetable salad. ERA MISMATCH risk: first written recipe ~1880s–1903

mousse-chocolat.notes: Updated from Q1055851; Q1381277 is chocolate mousse — dessert

terrine-de-legumes.notes: Updated from Q740861 (wrong — returned 'Martin Petrášek'); Q14624645 confirmed via Wikipedia cross-link on 'Terrine' article. General terrine item — no specific QID for vegetable terrine.

gratin-dauphinois.notes: Updated from Q1537368; Q1647122 is gratin dauphinois — French dish. ERA MISMATCH expected: documented 1788

tarte-tatin.notes: Updated from Q189966; Q1147946 is tarte Tatin — pastry with caramelised fruit that originated in France. ERA MISMATCH expected: Tatin sisters ~1880–1910

ratatouille-contemporary.notes: Updated from Q178115; Q183095 is ratatouille — French dish. Narrative variant of ratatouille-nouvelle — same underlying dish QID

pain-perdu.notes: Updated from Q745530; Q197973 is French toast — bread soaked in beaten eggs and then fried

foie-gras-contemporary.notes: Updated from Q83620 ('voie de communication' — wrong!); Q34807 confirmed via Wikipedia cross-link. Ancient dish; contemporary designation is a narrative framing.

soupe-de-truffe.notes: Updated from Q3490985 ('Qelelevu' — wrong!); Q16677587 confirmed via Wikipedia cross-link. Bocuse 1975 creation for Valéry Giscard d'Estaing. ERA INTENTIONAL OVERRIDE: Wikidata year 1975 maps to 'nouvelle' but project assigns 'contemporary' — dish is framed as a living contemporary symbol, not a historical nouvelle artifact. Do not auto-correct era.

oeuf-parfait.notes: Updated from Q1053860; Q97179868 is œuf parfait (no description). Sous-vide egg technique may not have a canonical standalone Wikidata item

madeleine.notes: Updated from Q204189; Q604408 is madeleine — type of cake traditional from France. ERA MISMATCH expected: 18th-century cake (~1755)
